title: mentha gentilis var. resinosa
intro: Mentha gentilis var. resinosa, also known as Scotch mint, is a variant of the peppermint plant that is native to Europe and has a characteristic resinous oil. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to relieve digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems due to its menthol and menthone content. The health benefits of Scotch mint include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe sore throats, ease nausea, and promote relaxation. Scotch mint can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or essential oil, which can be used in aromatherapy or added to topical creams and ointments for various health benefits.
